status: agent-proposed, 2026-06-19; every label pending maintainer ratification (single labeler). Regenerated against the HEAD ratified answer key.
supersedes: charness-artifacts/eval-trust/2026-06-10-recommendedproof-facet-gold-set-proposal.{md,json} (measured against packet 2a75d88c, 108 commits behind HEAD; ~half its sampled claimIds no longer exist at HEAD). Facet vocabulary, the route discriminators, and the verdict scheme carry forward as METHOD; the labels are re-derived on the current population.
purpose: D3 (regenerated): measure the HEAD answer key's per-claim recommendedProof accuracy at facet granularity, and harvest the per-facet routing real claims need, as design input + test fixture for per-facet recommendedProof wiring. Stratified by the blind extractor's per-claim recommendedProof (the analog of the old heuristic tag); the ratified maintainerVerdict (accept/relabel) is the per-claim ground truth the facet view is checked against.
answerKey: charness-artifacts/eval-trust/goldset-v2-reextract-head/ (375 ratified entries; accept 346 / relabel 19 / not-a-claim 10; gold = accept+relabel = 365). Operative template b922fd5d; source anchor 628ccc7; ratified 2026-06-18 (hitl-reextract-v2head-20260618 + Option C + recuration).
selectionRule: From goldset-v2-reextract-head/gold-set-proposal.json entries with maintainerVerdict in {accept,relabel}, per blind agentLabels.recommendedProof: sort by claimId, pick indices round(i*(n-1)/11) for i in 0..11 (12 per route). Gold-population distribution: deterministic 277, human-auditable 51, cautilus-eval 37.
ratifiedRulesApplied: The decompositions apply the HEAD answer key's ratified R6/R12 discriminators, which postdate the 2026-06-10 D3 set. R6/R12: an ownership/boundary/sequencing assignment, or a static repo-owned check that could prove a capability, routes DETERMINISTIC -- the actual agent behavior is a DIFFERENT claim's content and is not split off as a phantom eval facet. This collapses several D3-era 'too-coarse ownership splits' back to exact-deterministic (e.g. cet-18).

verdictDefinitions.exact: every substantive facet routes to the blind per-claim route's class
verdictDefinitions.too-coarse: the dominant facet matches the blind route but at least one substantive facet routes elsewhere
verdictDefinitions.wrong: the dominant facet routes to a different class than the blind route

entries.summary: Default discovery starts from entry surfaces and follows only repo-local Markdown links.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: discovery traversal starts from the configured entry surfaces
traversal follows only repo-local Markdown links (does not follow external/non-md links)

entries.note: Pure traversal behavior; both facets mechanically checkable.

entries.summary: Running `discover claims` directly without the Cautilus Agent emits the deterministic heuristic baseline (extractionMode: heuristic) with no model calls, the reproducible floor for no-model environments, CI regeneration, and control tests.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: `discover claims` without the agent emits extractionMode: heuristic
the heuristic path makes no model calls (negative)
output is reproducible (same input -> same packet; CI/control-test floor)

entries.note: Reproducible no-model baseline; all facets deterministic.

entries.summary: discover claims validate emits cautilus.claim_validation_report.v1 for packet shape and evidence-ref checks without mutating claims.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: validate emits cautilus.claim_validation_report.v1
validate performs packet-shape and evidence-ref checks
validate does not mutate claims (negative)

entries.note: Schema + negative non-mutation; deterministic.

entries.summary: The extraction seam mirrors the review seam: the binary emits a deterministic input packet, the agent does the model-backed work, and the binary validates and applies the result.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction + audience/group/proof labels correct; reservation recorded (kept accept under R5/R11): could be read as a legitimate deterministic seam-shape umbrella rather than pure framing. Tier T2. [maintainer override @ratify-2026-06-18: not-a-claim -> accept: R8 seam-mirroring is a structural composition with its own checkable content, not pure R9 framing]

proposedFacets.facet: binary emits a deterministic extraction-input packet
the model-backed extraction step is reserved to the agent (seam boundary)
binary validates and applies the result (apply-extraction)

entries.note: R6 reconciliation: the 'agent does the model-backed work' clause is a SEAM BOUNDARY (deterministic), not a behavior facet; the agent's extraction quality is a separate claim's content (R6 discriminator). The D3-era pattern of splitting ownership into [det-stated]+[eval-behavior] is superseded here by the ratified R6 rule. Ratified accept (maintainer override not-a-claim->accept: R8 seam-mirroring has checkable content).

entries.summary: Refresh binds re-extraction to changed sources via the recorded git commit plus per-source content hashes.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: refresh records the git commit + per-source content hashes
re-extraction is bound to changed sources (unchanged carry forward)

entries.note: Change-binding via recorded hashes; deterministic.

entries.summary: Every claim must carry at least one excerpt copied exactly from the source text, with its source path and a line locator.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: each claim carries >=1 verbatim excerpt copied exactly from source (anchoring)
each excerpt carries source path + line locator

entries.note: Verbatim-anchoring rule; deterministic (this rule IS the anchoring validator).

entries.summary: The discover claims status summary includes evidenceSatisfaction so satisfied claims and their evidence refs are visible without reading the full claim packet first.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: the status summary includes evidenceSatisfaction with satisfied claims + evidence refs

entries.note: 'visible without reading the full packet' is a deterministic consequence of the field being in the summary, not an independently-evaluable usability facet (contrast D3 cdw-382 'make curation efficient', which could fail even when present).

entries.summary: When the adapter declares workspace_prepare_command_template, Cautilus runs that consumer-owned prepare command once before the first turn.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: when workspace_prepare_command_template is declared, Cautilus runs it (conditional)
it runs exactly once, before the first turn (timing/count)

entries.note: Conditional prepare-command behavior; deterministic.

entries.summary: cautilus doctor adapter resolves and validates the adapter.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: doctor adapter resolves the adapter path
doctor adapter validates the adapter schema

entries.note: Resolve + validate; deterministic.

entries.summary: For dev/repo and dev/skill, a non-fixture coding-agent runtime may report the adapter-declared coding-agent-messaging class while preserving the smoke assessment as assessment metadata, but only when the observed packet reports the matching runtime telemetry.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: for dev/repo and dev/skill, a non-fixture runtime may report the adapter-declared messaging class
the smoke assessment is preserved as assessment metadata
this applies only when the observed packet reports matching runtime telemetry (negative guard)

entries.note: Conditional reporting with a telemetry guard; all deterministic including the negative.

entries.summary: Evaluation uses two top-level surfaces: dev for AI-assisted development work such as repo contracts, tools, and skills, and app for AI-powered product behavior such as chat, prompt, and service responses.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: evaluation exposes exactly two top-level surfaces (dev, app)
the dev/app surface definitions match the implemented surface taxonomy

entries.note: Surface taxonomy is a static contract.

entries.summary: Cautilus installs as a machine-level binary, but its agent-facing surface is intentionally repo-local.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: Cautilus installs as a machine-level binary (install path)
the agent-facing surface (skill) is repo-local, not machine-global (boundary)

entries.note: Install/packaging structure; both checkable.

entries.summary: Seeding classification hints is Cautilus Agent onboarding work: scan entry documents for rejected-alternative/non-goal conventions and claim vocabulary, propose hint lists with before/after extraction counts, and let the maintainer ratify them into the adapter before the first broad discovery.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: hint-seeding is assigned to the agent onboarding step (boundary)
maintainer ratification is sequenced before the first broad discovery (gate)
the agent actually scans entry docs and proposes sound hint lists with before/after counts

entries.note: The substantive promise is the agent producing sound hint proposals (eval); the assignment and the ratification-gate sequencing are deterministic boundaries.

entries.summary: The primary user flow starts from an agent session, not from a human reading a CLI manual.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction + audience/group/proof labels correct; reservation recorded (kept accept under R5/R11): borderline R16/R9 positioning premise for the numbered flow. Tier T1.

proposedFacets.facet: design-stance/positioning: the primary flow is agent-first, not CLI-manual-first
the agent-session entry path exists as the documented primary entrypoint

entries.note: DIVERGES from ratified accept-as-eval. No substantive agent-behavior facet to evaluate: this is an R16/R9 positioning premise (proven by reading the design) plus a structural entrypoint fact. The ratified note itself flagged it 'borderline R16/R9 positioning premise', which supports the human/det reading over eval. cautilus-eval over-assignment (pattern 1).

entries.summary: The parent Cautilus Agent merges subagent review results and keeps review provenance in the packet.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction + audience/group/proof labels correct; reservation recorded (kept accept under R5/R11): merge+provenance leans structural; agent-merge behavior keeps eval defensible. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: the parent agent actually merges subagent review results (orchestration behavior)
review provenance is kept in the packet (schema/structure)

entries.note: Ratified accept with reservation 'merge+provenance leans structural'. The provenance-in-packet facet is deterministic; the agent-merge is the eval facet. Dominance debatable per the ratified reservation.

entries.summary: The Cautilus Agent curates the discovery skeleton into the workflow a user expects when asking an agent to use Cautilus on a repo: which claims exist, which are covered, which need scenarios, which surfaces need alignment.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T1.

proposedFacets.facet: the agent actually curates the skeleton into the workflow a user expects (curation behavior)
the underlying data (claims exist / covered / needs-scenario / surface alignment) is computed in the packet

entries.note: Genuine agent-curation eval claim with a deterministic data-availability facet underneath.

entries.summary: Verifying the Cautilus Agent flow itself is later Cautilus-eval-fixture work over the skill surface and is not a prerequisite for the comparison.
entries.ratifiedNote: Relabel — proof route cautilus-eval -> human-auditable (Option C re-adjudication 2026-06-18, re-aimed from the prior eval->deterministic): a sequencing/scope assertion with no substance check; reading the contract is the proof (2/3 adjudicators). agentLabels left as-extracted. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: sequencing/scope assertion: skill-flow verification is deferred and not a prerequisite for the comparison

entries.note: MATCHES ratified relabel (eval->human-auditable, Option C): a scope/sequencing assertion with no substance check; reading the contract is the proof. Confirms the per-claim relabel at facet granularity.

entries.summary: v2 routes recommendedProof to deterministic whenever a static repo-owned check could prove a capability claim, instead of defaulting it to human-auditable.
entries.ratifiedNote: Relabel — proof route cautilus-eval -> deterministic (R6/R12: enabler is a static/structural contract or ownership/boundary assignment, checkable in principle (R3), not an agent-behavior outcome). agentLabels left as-extracted for the answer key; correction recorded here. Basis: describes a routing rule whose downstream effect is agent behavior, which can tempt eval. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: the v2 template's routing rule (det whenever a static check could prove the claim) is encoded in the operative template, checkable by template content + a routing test on synthetic claims

entries.note: MATCHES ratified relabel (eval->deterministic, R6/R12). This is the 'meta template-content read as behavioral' class -- the cet documented-content under-route residual measured at 8.75% in MEASUREMENT.proof-route.md. The facet view confirms it routes deterministic.

entries.summary: When the agent is unsure whether text is a claim, it emits the claim with verificationReadiness blocked and an entry in unresolvedQuestions instead of silently dropping it.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept (Option C re-adjudication 2026-06-18): the blind route cautilus-eval was correct; the prior relabel to deterministic was a key error. The agent emit-blocked-when-unsure behavior is settled only by agent execution (3/3 adjudicators). Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: the agent actually emits uncertain text as a claim with verificationReadiness blocked + an unresolvedQuestions entry, instead of silently dropping it
the schema supports verificationReadiness=blocked and unresolvedQuestions entries (structure)

entries.note: MATCHES ratified accept (Option C: blind eval correct; the emit-blocked-when-unsure agent judgment is settled only by execution). The schema-support facet is the deterministic half.

entries.summary: The agent does not voluntarily run review gates on vague unease alone, but a review gate that has entered scope is treated as already delegated.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: the agent does NOT run review gates on vague unease alone (negative behavior)
the agent treats an in-scope review gate as already delegated (positive behavior)

entries.note: Genuine dual agent-behavior claim; both facets are semantic routing-discipline outcomes (the reasoning-soundness judge's domain).

entries.summary: specdown-flavored language (docs/specs/**/*.spec.md, run:shell, doctest, check:jq, specdown HTML report, report.json) must first be discovered through the Charness support/specdown skill: check support/specdown in the find-skills inventory and read its syntax/best-practices/cli/report reference before treating it as plain Markdown/HITL work.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: the agent actually routes specdown-flavored work through support/specdown (find-skills inventory first) before treating it as plain Markdown/HITL
the specdown trigger vocabulary (spec.md, run:shell, doctest, check:jq, report) is an enumerable static list

entries.note: A routing claim (same family as the reasoning-soundness prototype): the routing soundness is eval; the trigger vocabulary is a deterministic list.

entries.summary: Cautilus ships a GEPA-style bounded prompt search seam above the one-shot improver: multi-generation reflective mutation, protected reevaluation, frontier-promotion review reuse, checkpoint feedback reinjection, bounded merge synthesis, and Pareto-style frontier selection.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T1.

proposedFacets.facet: the binary ships the GEPA-style search seam with the named mechanisms (multi-gen mutation, protected reeval, frontier-promotion reuse, checkpoint reinjection, merge synthesis, Pareto selection)
the bounded search actually improves prompts across generations (the capability outcome)

entries.note: The flagship improvement promise. Dominance debatable, leaning deterministic: under R12 as written, the existence of the six named mechanisms is a static capability-existence check -- the STRONGER reading -> dominant-deterministic, which flips the verdict to 'wrong'. Only the does-it-actually-improve-prompts outcome is genuinely eval. Marked dominant-eval to match the ratified accept-as-eval, but the maintainer should weigh the R12 'ships X' reading that makes it wrong.

entries.summary: The Cautilus Agent curates the discovery packet against the repo: reduce false positives, raise likely missing public promises, and separate in-scope discovery bugs from out-of-scope narrative gaps.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T1.

proposedFacets.facet: the agent actually reduces false positives, raises likely-missing public promises, and separates in-scope discovery bugs from out-of-scope narrative gaps (curation quality)

entries.note: Pure agent-curation-quality eval claim (same family as cdw-6); no separable deterministic facet.

entries.summary: The dogfood run turned the question of whether the agent read and followed the repo instructions from transcript judgment into a reproducible packet with artifacts another maintainer can reopen.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction + audience/group/proof labels correct; reservation recorded (kept accept under R5/R11): phrased as dogfood-specific but R18 carves the general turn-judgment-into-reproducible-packet capability as the real claim. Tier T1.

proposedFacets.facet: workflow runs produce a reproducible packet with reopenable artifacts (structure)
the packet actually captures whether the agent read+followed the repo instructions (the dogfood subject)
another maintainer can in practice reopen the artifacts and find them sufficient (acceptance)

entries.note: R18 carves the general 'turn transcript-judgment into a reproducible packet' capability as the real claim. Composite: reproducible-packet (det) + following-behavior capture (eval) + reopen acceptance (human). Dominance debatable (the reproducible-packet capability could be read as the det dominant, cf. D3 reviewable-artifacts-spec-3); marked dominant-eval to match ratified accept.

entries.summary: Claim packets are high-recall proof-planning inputs, not the primary human review surface once the maintainer is judging product meaning, duplication, audience fit, or next-action grouping.
entries.ratifiedNote: Relabel — proof route human-auditable -> deterministic (R6/R12: enabler is a static/structural contract or ownership/boundary assignment, checkable in principle (R3), not an agent-behavior outcome). agentLabels left as-extracted for the answer key; correction recorded here. Basis: could read as R16 identity/positioning premise instead. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: the packet's role/boundary (high-recall proof-planning input, not the primary human review surface once the maintainer judges meaning/dup/audience/grouping) is a stated, checkable boundary

entries.note: MATCHES ratified relabel (human->deterministic, R6/R12 boundary assignment). The ratified note flags 'could read as R16 identity/positioning premise' (human), so dominance is debatable; the relabel chose deterministic. human-auditable hiding a deterministic boundary (pattern 2).

entries.summary: When a standing gate has landed, escape hatches are re-evaluated: temporary enforcement escapes (e.g. `Must*` helpers) are checked for whether they can be deleted once the gate is in.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: operating practice: when a standing gate lands, escape hatches (e.g. Must* helpers) are re-evaluated for deletion

entries.note: A working-pattern practice; the claim is the re-evaluation discipline, not a specific deletion. Genuinely human.

entries.summary: Stable commands an operator directly expects via `cautilus ...`, surfaces a release artifact calls officially supported, and surfaces whose error contract will be strongly pinned long-term default to being pulled up into the Go CLI.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: promotion-policy criteria (operator-expected via `cautilus`, release-officially-supported, error-contract strongly pinned -> default to Go CLI) is a design judgment

entries.note: Promotion-policy criteria; the judgment of whether a surface meets them is human.

entries.summary: Go promotion trigger: the adapter indirection (e.g. `consumer_command_template`) stays stable across two or three sessions.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: Go-promotion trigger: the adapter indirection stays stable across 2-3 sessions (a judgment criterion)

entries.note: Promotion-trigger judgment criterion; human.

entries.summary: Go promotion trigger: it becomes worth including in binary-level smoke / help text / command discovery.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: Go-promotion trigger: it becomes worth including in binary-level smoke / help text / command discovery (a judgment criterion)

entries.note: Promotion-trigger judgment criterion; human.

entries.summary: One counterweight pass runs right after premortem: a single counterweight agent is asked to flag over-worry/YAGNI/premature improvement, and it classifies each finding into one of four buckets (must-fix before release, cheap to fold in, over-worry to ignore, valid but defer).
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: the counterweight-pass practice runs once right after premortem (operating pattern)
the four-bucket finding taxonomy (must-fix / cheap-to-fold / over-worry / valid-but-defer) is a documented, enumerable structure

entries.note: The practice/sequencing is human; the four-bucket taxonomy is a checkable documented structure.

entries.summary: Iterative premortem: after partially applying round-1 results, a round 2 runs again on the new decisions that arose from those changes.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: operating pattern: iterative premortem runs a round 2 on the new decisions arising from partially-applied round-1 results

entries.note: Process pattern; human.

entries.summary: Rejected or not-done decisions live only in explicit decision sections with `Fixed Decisions` / `Deliberately Not Doing` style headings and in goal artifacts.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: rejected/not-done decisions appear ONLY under explicit decision-section headings (Fixed Decisions / Deliberately Not Doing) and in goal artifacts -- a checkable placement rule
the editorial judgment of what counts as a 'rejected decision' worth recording

entries.note: DIVERGES from ratified accept-as-human. The dominant facet IS the non_claim_section_headings discipline: a placement rule a scanner enforces (rejected-decision statements must not appear outside those headings) -- the live classification hint family. human-auditable hiding a deterministic placement rule (pattern 2). High-value review item: directly relevant to classification_hints wiring.

entries.summary: Cross-cutting concerns are not exposed in AOP terms in public docs: internal design uses AOP/dominant-decomposition warnings, but public docs speak in concrete contracts and evidence routes (e.g. 'every workflow leaves reviewable artifacts', 'missing evidence remains visible' rather than 'aspect').
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T3.

proposedFacets.facet: public docs do not contain AOP/aspect vocabulary (negative lexical check)
public docs speak in concrete contracts and evidence routes (positive style quality)

entries.note: DIVERGES from ratified accept-as-human. The headline is a negative lexical constraint (no AOP terms in public docs) that a scanner can enforce -> deterministic; the positive 'speak concretely' is the human-judged half. Dominance debatable (the design-communication philosophy could be read as dominant-human). Surfaced as a facet-vs-per-claim granularity divergence.

entries.summary: When a v1 surface name leaks archetype vocabulary, the default is catalog-level deprecation (alias + silent normalization) rather than a schema bump; a schema bump is only for when there is a real reason to force consumer migration.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept — extraction correct; audience/group/proof labels correct. Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: the deprecation policy (alias + silent normalization by default; schema bump only to force consumer migration) is a versioning design judgment
the alias + silent-normalization mechanism exists and works (catalog-level deprecation)

entries.note: The when-to-bump policy is human judgment; the alias/normalization mechanism is a checkable deterministic facet.

entries.summary: Cautilus does not freeze one prompt string as the contract; it treats the behavior under evaluation as the contract (intent-first).
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept (Option C re-adjudication 2026-06-18): the blind route human-auditable was correct; the prior relabel to cautilus-eval over-read the intent-first design philosophy as behavior. Behavior-as-contract is a design stance proven by reading the design (2/3 adjudicators). Tier T1.

proposedFacets.facet: design stance: Cautilus treats the behavior under evaluation as the contract, intent-first (not a frozen prompt string)
the eval mechanism does not gate pass/fail on a single frozen prompt-string equality (negative, checkable)

entries.note: MATCHES ratified accept (Option C: blind human correct; prior eval-relabel over-read the philosophy as behavior). Dominant is the intent-first design stance (human); the no-frozen-prompt negative is a deterministic facet.

entries.summary: The dogfood run exposed that routing proof is not backend subagent capability proof, and keeping that distinction in the packet prevented over-claiming what had been verified.
entries.ratifiedNote: Accept (Option C re-adjudication 2026-06-18): the blind route human-auditable was correct; the prior relabel to deterministic over-applied R3. A dogfood retro narrative about what a run exposed, no substance check (3/3 adjudicators). Tier T2.

proposedFacets.facet: retro narrative: the dogfood run exposed that routing proof is not backend subagent-capability proof, and keeping that distinction in the packet prevented over-claiming

entries.note: MATCHES ratified accept (Option C): a dogfood retro narrative about what a run exposed, no substance check and nothing to re-run. Genuinely human.
